# TerraNext

> An opinionated Terraform module to host your Next.js app on AWS without breaking the bank on compute. Built on OpenNext.

TerraNext is an open-source Terraform module that deploys Next.js applications to AWS using the OpenNext build output. It provisions CloudFront, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, and SQS to give you a fully serverless, cost-effective hosting stack with full infrastructure control.

## Key Features

- Full control over every AWS resource via Terraform or OpenTofu
- Serverless architecture: CloudFront + Lambda + S3
- ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) support via DynamoDB and SQS
- Image optimisation via a dedicated Lambda function
- Optional warmer function to reduce cold starts
- Response streaming support
- DNSSEC, HSTS, and CORS configuration out of the box

## Quick Start

1. Build your Next.js app with OpenNext: `npx @opennextjs/aws@latest build`
2. Add the TerraNext module to your Terraform configuration
3. Run `terraform apply`

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/terranext-dev/terraform-aws-opennext
- Website: https://terranext.dev
